steps.text: Let me show you. Emma has 20 books. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. That's 4 symbols. Noah has 25: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. That's 5 symbols. Olivia has 40: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. That's 8 symbols. Skip-counting by 5s tells you exactly how many symbols to place.

params.description: Emma and Olivia rows highlight.

steps.text: Let me show you. Emma's row has 4 symbols. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. Olivia's row has 8 symbols. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Now subtract: 40 minus 20 equals 20. Emma read 20 fewer books than Olivia. When you compare, you count by 5s for each row, then find the difference between those totals.

steps.text: Scale of 5 uses the fewest symbols. When each symbol represents MORE books, you need FEWER symbols. That's why bigger scales are useful when numbers get large.
